assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md identifies both patterns: (1) Error handling section: 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs' and 'Errors are wrapped with .context("descriptive message") for meaningful error chains'; (2) Module structure section: 'Each domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ tripartite structure'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1 parsed fields include 'Target Branch: main'. A dedicated subsection '### Target Branch extraction' states: 'Target Branch is main. This value will be used in Step 5 (branch creation) and Step 10 (PR base).' Step 5 confirms 'Target Branch is main.' Step 10 PR creation uses '--base main' with note 'The --base main flag explicitly targets the Target Branch extracted in Step 1.'

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1.5 is titled 'Verify Description Integrity' and states: 'Fetched comments on TC-9201 via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201).' It then notes: 'No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced.' and 'Per the backward compatibility rules in shared/description-digest-protocol.md, a missing digest comment is a non-blocking warning. Proceeding with implementation normally.'
